project.name: claude-agents-dashboard
project.description: Live monitor for parallel Claude Code sessions

nodes.name: Header.tsx
nodes.summary: Top header bar for the Sessions view: shows generation timestamp, total session/active counts, and (when enabled) 5h/weekly usage-limit bars with per-window color-coded progress.

nodes.name: Header
nodes.summary: Root header component rendering the generated-at time, session totals, and delegating to UsageBars for the rate-limit display.

nodes.name: UsageBars
nodes.summary: Builds the 5h/Week bar list from usage data and renders one UsageBar per window, or a token-expired hint per usageStatus.

nodes.name: UsageBar
nodes.summary: Renders a single rate-limit progress bar with a color level derived from utilization percentage and a formatted reset time.

nodes.name: MultiSelect.tsx
nodes.summary: Generic dropdown multi-select control with click-outside and Escape-to-close handling, used by the Toolbar for project/status filters.

nodes.name: MultiSelect
nodes.summary: Stateful dropdown component managing open/close state, outside-click/Escape dismissal via refs and effects, and toggling selected options.

nodes.name: SessionDetail.tsx
nodes.summary: Expanded per-session detail panel showing subagent activity: polls session detail data and renders a timeline of agent jobs with token/duration metadata.

nodes.name: timeRange
nodes.summary: Computes the earliest-start/latest-activity time range across a set of agent jobs, defaulting the end to now.

nodes.name: agentMeta
nodes.summary: Builds the compact metadata string (tokens, tool uses, duration) shown next to each agent job entry.

nodes.name: SessionDetail
nodes.summary: Fetches live session detail via useSessionDetail and renders the agent-job timeline, guarding against missing/loading data.

nodes.name: SessionList.tsx
nodes.summary: Renders the filtered/sorted list of sessions as expandable rows, owning which row IDs are currently expanded.

nodes.name: SessionList
nodes.summary: Maps sessions to SessionRow components and tracks per-row expansion state via a Set, toggled on row click.

nodes.name: SessionRow.tsx
nodes.summary: Single collapsible session row: status dot, project/branch/model info, token usage, and last-activity time, expanding to show SessionDetail.

nodes.name: SessionRow
nodes.summary: Renders a session's summary line with formatted token counts and relative time, expanding to embed SessionDetail when selected.

nodes.name: SessionsView.tsx
nodes.summary: Top-level Sessions tab: owns the 3s live-session poll and persisted Toolbar filter/sort view, composing Header, Toolbar, and SessionList.

nodes.name: SessionsView
nodes.summary: Combines the live useSessions poll with the persisted view state and applyView filtering/sorting to render Header, Toolbar, and SessionList.

nodes.name: Toolbar.tsx
nodes.summary: Filter/sort control bar for the session list: project and status MultiSelects, activity-window and sort-key selects, wired to the persisted View state.

nodes.name: Toolbar
nodes.summary: Renders project/status MultiSelects plus activity-window and sort dropdowns, calling onChange with an updated View on any control change.

nodes.name: useSessionDetail.ts
nodes.summary: Custom hook that polls the on-demand GET /api/sessions/:id endpoint every few seconds to fetch a single session's subagent detail.

nodes.name: useSessionDetail
nodes.summary: Sets up an interval-based fetch of a single session's detail by id, updating state on each poll and cleaning up the interval on unmount.

nodes.name: useSessions.ts
nodes.summary: Custom hook that polls GET /api/sessions every 3 seconds, powering the live monitor's core session list.

nodes.name: useSessions
nodes.summary: Fetches the sessions list on an interval, storing the SessionsResponse in state and clearing the interval on unmount.

nodes.name: filterSort.ts
nodes.summary: Pure filter/sort logic and shared constants (status labels/order, activity windows, default View) for the Toolbar and SessionsView.

nodes.name: distinctProjects
nodes.summary: Returns the sorted list of unique project names present across sessions, used to populate the project MultiSelect.

nodes.name: compare
nodes.summary: Comparator for sorting two sessions by a given View sort key (project, status, tokens, etc.).

nodes.name: applyView
nodes.summary: Applies the current View's project/status/activity-window filters and sort key/direction to the session list.

nodes.name: format.ts
nodes.summary: Shared formatting helpers for token counts, durations, relative timestamps, and rate-limit reset times, used across Header, SessionRow, and SessionDetail.

nodes.name: fmtTok
nodes.summary: Formats a raw token count into a compact human-readable string (e.g. 1.2M, 340k).

nodes.name: fmtDuration
nodes.summary: Formats a millisecond duration into a short human-readable string (seconds/minutes/hours).

nodes.name: formatAgo
nodes.summary: Formats a millisecond elapsed time into a relative 'x ago' style string for last-activity display.

nodes.name: formatResetTime
nodes.summary: Formats an ISO reset timestamp into a compact local time (and date, if not today) for usage-limit bars.

nodes.name: DetailPane.tsx
nodes.summary: Detail pane component for the Management tab that renders the selected item's content, dispatching to FileViewer, HookDetail, or MarkdownViewer based on the entry's badge/kind.

nodes.name: typeLabel
nodes.summary: Formats a badge/title string into a human-readable type label, stripping known suffixes.

nodes.name: DetailPane
nodes.summary: React component that renders the header and content area for the currently selected management entry, choosing the right viewer sub-component by entry kind.

nodes.name: FileViewer.tsx
nodes.summary: Generic raw-file viewer used in the Management detail pane; fetches file content via useFileContent and optionally pretty-prints JSON.

nodes.name: prettyJson
nodes.summary: Attempts to parse and re-stringify content as indented JSON, falling back to the raw string on parse failure.

nodes.name: FileViewer
nodes.summary: React component that loads a file's content via useFileContent and displays it (optionally pretty-printed) with a token-count footer.

nodes.name: HookDetail.tsx
nodes.summary: Detail view for a hook config entry in the Management tab, showing hook metadata and an expandable view of its declaring file via FileViewer.

nodes.name: HookDetail
nodes.summary: React component that renders a hook's event/matcher/command details and a toggle to reveal the declaring settings file contents.

nodes.name: ItemList.tsx
nodes.summary: Filterable, grouped list pane for the Management tab showing config items (skills, agents, commands, hooks, etc.) with collapsible subgroups and a filter box.

nodes.name: collapsedByDefault
nodes.summary: Determines whether a given group/subgroup combination should start collapsed by default.

nodes.name: badgeText
nodes.summary: Strips a leading 'plugin:' prefix from a badge string for cleaner display.

nodes.name: ItemList
nodes.summary: React component rendering the filterable, grouped list of management entries with persisted collapse state and selection callbacks.

nodes.name: ManagementView.tsx
nodes.summary: Top-level three-pane Management tab view wiring together ScopeMenu, ItemList, and DetailPane, backed by useManagementIndex/useProjectScope data hooks.
nodes.languageNotes: Default-exported React component that composes the three-pane layout described in the project's management.md rule doc.

nodes.name: ManagementView
nodes.summary: React component that manages selected scope/entry state, derives filtered entry groups via buildEntries/filterEntries, and renders the three-pane Management layout.

nodes.name: MarkdownViewer.tsx
nodes.summary: Markdown file viewer for the Management detail pane; fetches file content, parses YAML frontmatter, and displays metadata plus token count.

nodes.name: MarkdownViewer
nodes.summary: React component that loads a markdown file, parses its frontmatter via parseFrontmatter, and renders metadata keys plus the raw body with a token count footer.

nodes.name: ScopeMenu.tsx
nodes.summary: Left-pane scope selector for the Management tab, listing Global plus recently-active projects for the user to switch between.

nodes.name: ScopeMenu
nodes.summary: React component rendering clickable scope entries (Global + recent projects) with the currently selected scope highlighted.

nodes.name: useManagement.ts
nodes.summary: Data-fetching hooks for the Management tab: fetch the global ManagementIndex, a project's ScopeConfig, and individual file contents, each with lazy caching.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses ref-held Maps as request-level caches so re-selecting the same project/file avoids re-fetching, matching the 'lazy fetch on click, cache in ref' policy from management.md.

nodes.name: useManagementIndex
nodes.summary: Hook that fetches GET /api/management once (or on refreshKey change) and returns the ManagementIndex with loading/error state.

nodes.name: useProjectScope
nodes.summary: Hook that lazily fetches a project's ScopeConfig from GET /api/management/project, caching results per dirName in a ref-held Map.

nodes.name: useFileContent
nodes.summary: Hook that lazily fetches a file's content from GET /api/management/file, caching results per path in a ref-held Map.

nodes.name: usePersistedState.ts
nodes.summary: Generic useState replacement that lazily reads/parses a localStorage value on init and persists on every change, failing open on bad JSON or a throwing localStorage.
nodes.languageNotes: Fail-open localStorage wrapper: shallow-merges object values over the passed default so older stored shapes still gain new fields, per view-persistence.md.

nodes.name: usePersistedState
nodes.summary: Generic hook returning a [value, setValue] pair backed by localStorage under a given key, with fail-open error handling and shallow-merge over a fallback default.

nodes.name: managementEntries.ts
nodes.summary: Pure entry-normalization logic for the Management tab: builds grouped ManagementEntry lists from a ScopeConfig and filters them by a search query.
nodes.languageNotes: Kept pure and unit-tested per management.md ('Entry normalization is pure ... unit-tested').

nodes.name: fileKind
nodes.summary: Classifies a file path's kind (e.g. markdown vs JSON) based on its extension.

nodes.name: itemSubgroup
nodes.summary: Derives a subgroup label (e.g. plugin name) from an item's source string.

nodes.name: bySubgroupThenLabel
nodes.summary: Comparator sorting entries first by subgroup, then alphabetically by label.

nodes.name: fromItems
nodes.summary: Converts a list of raw scope items into normalized, sorted ManagementEntry objects, optionally grouped by subgroup.

nodes.name: fromHooks
nodes.summary: Converts hook configs into normalized, sorted ManagementEntry objects for display in the item list.

nodes.name: buildEntries
nodes.summary: Builds the full set of grouped ManagementEntry lists (skills, agents, commands, rules, hooks, settings) from a ScopeConfig, tagging plugin-sourced items.

nodes.name: filterEntries
nodes.summary: Filters grouped entries by a case-insensitive substring match against label/sublabel for the search box.

nodes.name: frontmatter.ts
nodes.summary: Zero-dependency YAML-frontmatter subset parser shared between client and server, supporting key:value pairs and >/| block scalars, used to read skill/rule metadata.
nodes.languageNotes: Fail-open parser design (per lib/frontmatter.ts description in CLAUDE.md) — malformed frontmatter degrades gracefully rather than throwing.

nodes.name: unquote
nodes.summary: Strips surrounding single or double quotes from a frontmatter scalar value.

nodes.name: parseFrontmatter
nodes.summary: Parses a leading YAML frontmatter block (key:value pairs and >/| multi-line scalars) out of markdown text, returning metadata plus the remaining body.

nodes.name: api.ts
nodes.summary: Implements the HTTP handlers for /api/sessions, /api/sessions/:id, /api/management*, and /api/analytics, wiring the scanner, management, and analytics modules to JSON responses.

nodes.name: index.ts
nodes.summary: HTTP entry point for the dashboard server: routes API requests to api.ts handlers and static-serves the built client in production.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses import.meta.url === file://process.argv[1] as the ESM equivalent of require.main === module to gate server.listen().

nodes.name: analytics.ts
nodes.summary: Read-only reader that pairs each /kaizen-logged session (from the session-analytics log) with a live re-run of analyzeSession, producing the Analytics tab's report list.

nodes.name: config.ts
nodes.summary: Loads and normalizes runtime configuration (.env parsing, defaults, Docker detection) with precedence process.env > .env > defaults.

nodes.name: management.ts
nodes.summary: Config scanner for the Management tab: enumerates global/plugin/project Claude config (skills, agents, commands, rules, hooks, settings) and enforces the servable-path security allowlist for file reads.
nodes.languageNotes: collectServablePaths + readServableFile implement an explicit allowlist rather than prefix checks, deliberately excluding sensitive files like .credentials.json under the same ~/.claude root.

nodes.name: serveSessions
nodes.summary: Handles GET /api/sessions: runs scanSessions, attaches optional usage-limit data, and writes the JSON session snapshot response.

nodes.name: serveSessionDetail
nodes.summary: Handles GET /api/sessions/:id: resolves the transcript by id prefix and returns cached subagent job activity for that session.

nodes.name: serveManagementIndex
nodes.summary: Handles GET /api/management: returns the global ScopeConfig plus recently-active projects as the ManagementIndex payload.

nodes.name: serveManagementProject
nodes.summary: Handles GET /api/management/project: resolves a project by dirName from the enumerated recent-project list and returns its ScopeConfig.

nodes.name: serveManagementFile
nodes.summary: Handles GET /api/management/file: serves file content only for paths present in the servable-paths allowlist, enforcing the management security invariant.

nodes.name: serveAnalytics
nodes.summary: Handles GET /api/analytics: returns the last N /kaizen-logged session reports as the AnalyticsResponse payload.

nodes.name: serveStatic
nodes.summary: Serves a file from client/dist with SPA-style fallback to index.html, guarding against path traversal outside the dist root.

nodes.name: listReports
nodes.summary: Pairs each recent /kaizen lesson with its matching transcript (by id prefix) and a live analyzeSession() re-run to build the AnalyticsResponse report list.

nodes.name: parseEnv
nodes.summary: Parses a .env file's text content into a key/value map, stripping quotes and skipping comments/blank lines.

nodes.name: toPosInt
nodes.summary: Coerces a config value to a positive integer, falling back to a default when invalid.

nodes.name: toBool
nodes.summary: Coerces a config value to a boolean using common truthy/falsy string forms, falling back to a default.

nodes.name: isDockerContainer
nodes.summary: Detects whether the process is running inside a Docker container by checking for /.dockerenv, used to default skipProcScan.

nodes.name: loadConfig
nodes.summary: Builds the final runtime Config by merging process.env, a parsed .env file, and DEFAULTS, with process.env taking precedence.

nodes.name: claudeHome
nodes.summary: Resolves the ~/.claude directory path, defaulting to the current user's home directory.

nodes.name: readSkillsDir
nodes.summary: Reads a skills directory, parsing each skill's SKILL.md frontmatter into management item metadata.

nodes.name: readMdDir
nodes.summary: Recursively reads a directory of markdown-based config items (agents, commands, rules), parsing frontmatter and descriptions at a bounded depth.

nodes.name: resolveScriptPath
nodes.summary: Resolves a hook command's script path against a set of allowed roots, returning the normalized path only if it stays within an allowed root and is a real file.

nodes.name: readHooksConfig
nodes.summary: Parses a settings.json hooks object into normalized hook items, resolving each hook's script path via resolveScriptPath.

nodes.name: readPlugin
nodes.summary: Reads one installed plugin's full subtree (skills, agents, commands, rules, hooks) from its installPath for the global scope.

nodes.name: readPlugins
nodes.summary: Reads installed_plugins.json and loads each enabled plugin's config subtree via readPlugin.

nodes.name: memoryItems
nodes.summary: Filters a list of candidate CLAUDE.md paths down to the ones that actually exist as files, building memory management items.

nodes.name: settingsHooks
nodes.summary: Reads settings.json/settings.local.json for a scope and extracts their hooks via readHooksConfig.

nodes.name: readGlobalScope
nodes.summary: Assembles the full global ScopeConfig: ~/.claude skills/agents/commands/rules/hooks/CLAUDE.md plus every installed plugin's subtree, tagged plugin:<name>.

nodes.name: readProjectScope
nodes.summary: Assembles a single project's ScopeConfig from <cwd>/.claude/* plus the project root CLAUDE.md, tagged project.

nodes.name: listRecentProjects
nodes.summary: Derives the recent-projects list for the Management tab from transcript cwds, deduped by cwd and sorted newest-first.

nodes.name: resolveProject
nodes.summary: Looks up a single recent project by its enumerated dirName, never joining the raw dirName directly into a path.

nodes.name: collectServablePaths
nodes.summary: Enumerates the exact set of file paths the scanner discovered (global + all project scopes) to form the file-endpoint security allowlist.

nodes.name: readServableFile
nodes.summary: Reads a file's content only if its normalized path is present in the allowed set, capping content at FILE_CONTENT_CAP bytes.

nodes.name: scan.ts
nodes.summary: Core session scanner: enumerates transcripts under ~/.claude/projects, computes per-session status via transcript signals and the lsof process-liveness gate, and ranks sessions for the live monitor.
nodes.languageNotes: liveCwds shells out to lsof -c claude -a -d cwd to fail-open gate stale 'working' sessions to idle when no real CLI process is attached to that cwd.

nodes.name: sessionAnalyticsLog.ts
nodes.summary: Parses ~/.claude/session-analytics-log.md (one line per /kaizen run) into structured lesson records and resolves the most recent lesson for a given session id.

nodes.name: transcript.ts
nodes.summary: Tail-reads the last N KB of a session transcript (.jsonl) to derive token usage, context window, model, and current tool activity without parsing the whole file.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses a bounded tail-byte read (fs.readSync into a fixed buffer) instead of loading the whole transcript, keeping the 3s poll cheap even for huge session files.

nodes.name: vite.config.ts
nodes.summary: Vite dev-server configuration: proxies /api to the backend and reuses the backend's loadConfig() to pick the matching port; logs the LAN URL for phone access.

nodes.name: projectsRoot
nodes.summary: Resolves the ~/.claude/projects root directory holding all session transcripts.

nodes.name: decodeProjectName
nodes.summary: Decodes a transcript directory name back into a human-readable project name (last path segment).

nodes.name: listTranscripts
nodes.summary: Enumerates all .jsonl transcript files under the projects root with their ids, project dirs, and mtimes.

nodes.name: countClaudeProcesses
nodes.summary: Counts live `claude` CLI processes via `ps`/`pgrep`-style shell-out, used for header diagnostics.

nodes.name: liveCwds
nodes.summary: Shells out to lsof -c claude -a -d cwd to build the set of cwds with a live claude CLI process attached, fail-open (null) on error.

nodes.name: scanSessions
nodes.summary: Main scan entry point: enumerates recent transcripts, reads each one, computes per-session status (working/incomplete/idle/question) via the liveCwds gate and transcript signals, and ranks the result.

nodes.name: sessionAnalyticsLogPath
nodes.summary: Resolves the path to ~/.claude/session-analytics-log.md.

nodes.name: parseSessionAnalyticsLog
nodes.summary: Parses the session-analytics-log.md text into structured lesson records via a line regex.

nodes.name: readSessionAnalyticsLog
nodes.summary: Reads and parses the session-analytics log file from disk, fail-open on missing file.

nodes.name: lessonForSession
nodes.summary: Finds the lesson entry matching a given session id by prefix match.

nodes.name: recentLessons
nodes.summary: Returns the most recent N lessons, deduped by session id prefix, newest-first.

nodes.name: readTail
nodes.summary: Reads the last tailBytes of a file via a fixed-size buffer and fs.readSync, avoiding a full-file read for large transcripts.

nodes.name: usageTokens
nodes.summary: Extracts input/output/cache token counts from a transcript usage record, defaulting missing fields to 0.

nodes.name: resolveWindow
nodes.summary: Determines the effective context window size (standard vs large) based on token count, model name pattern, and env override.

nodes.name: windowLabel
nodes.summary: Formats a context window size into a short display label (e.g. '1M').

nodes.name: describeTool
nodes.summary: Builds a short human-readable description of a tool_use content block (subagent type, file path, pattern, or command) for the current-activity display.

nodes.name: readTranscript
nodes.summary: Core transcript reader: tail-reads recent JSONL records to derive tokens, model, context window, turn-completion state, question-waiting state, and the current tool activity description.

nodes.name: logHostLanIp
nodes.summary: Dev-server plugin hook that logs the host machine's LAN IP once the HTTP server starts, so a phone on the same wifi can reach the Vite dev server.

nodes.name: AnalyticsView.tsx
nodes.summary: Renders the Analytics tab: a list of /kaizen-logged session report cards with token totals, per-tool cost, subagent breakdown, and error signals. Default-exported and lazy-loaded by App.tsx.

nodes.name: AnalyticsView
nodes.summary: Top-level Analytics view component: calls the useAnalytics hook and maps returned reports to ReportCard elements, handling loading/error/empty states.

nodes.name: ReportCard
nodes.summary: Renders a single session analysis report card, showing token totals, duration, top tools, and top subagents by cost.

nodes.name: Metric
nodes.summary: Small presentational subcomponent rendering a labeled metric value, optionally flagged as a lead/warning stat.

nodes.name: useAnalytics.ts
nodes.summary: Client-only hook that fetches GET /api/analytics on mount and on manual refresh, exposing the AnalyticsResponse loading/error state to AnalyticsView.

nodes.name: useAnalytics
nodes.summary: React hook fetching /api/analytics with a refresh-key trigger; returns { data, loading, error } plus a refresh callback. No polling.

nodes.name: agents-cache.ts
nodes.summary: Incremental byte-offset cache over agents.ts's whole-file subagent parser, re-reading only newly appended transcript bytes on repeat calls; used by the on-demand GET /api/sessions/:id endpoint.
nodes.languageNotes: Maintains a Map-backed LRU-ish cache keyed by file path, storing byte offset and parser scan state so re-reads only process the tail delta instead of the whole transcript.

nodes.name: freshEntry
nodes.summary: Creates a new empty cache entry with a fresh scan state and zero byte offset.

nodes.name: consumeLine
nodes.summary: Parses one JSONL line buffer and folds its events into the running scan state via parseRecordEvents/applyEvent.

nodes.name: evictIfNeeded
nodes.summary: Evicts the oldest cache entry once the cache exceeds its configured size cap.

nodes.name: readAgentsCached
nodes.summary: Main exported entry point: stats the transcript file, reuses or invalidates the cache entry, reads only the new byte range since the last offset, incrementally parses it, and returns the accumulated AgentJob list.

nodes.name: _resetAgentsCache
nodes.summary: Test-only helper that clears the module-level cache map.

nodes.name: _agentsCacheSize
nodes.summary: Test-only helper that exposes the current cache size for assertions.

nodes.name: agents.ts
nodes.summary: Whole-file subagent transcript parser and reducer: extracts Task-tool launches, tool uses, and notification <usage> blocks from a JSONL transcript and reduces them into a list of AgentJob records with tokens/toolUses/duration.
nodes.languageNotes: Splits parsing into a pure event-extraction pass (parseRecordEvents) and a separate state-reducer pass (applyEvent/toAgentJobs), which agents-cache.ts reuses for its incremental variant.

nodes.name: isAgentLaunch
nodes.summary: Detects whether a tool-use block represents a Task (subagent launch) invocation.

nodes.name: contentText
nodes.summary: Extracts plain text from a message content block, handling both string and array-of-blocks shapes.

nodes.name: toolResultText
nodes.summary: Extracts concatenated text from a tool_result content block.

nodes.name: finiteOrNull
nodes.summary: Coerces a value to a finite number or null.

nodes.name: intFromMatch
nodes.summary: Runs a regex against text and parses the first capture group as an integer.

nodes.name: parseRecordEvents
nodes.summary: Pure parser that inspects one transcript JSONL record and emits zero or more agent lifecycle events (launch/tool-use/tool-result/usage-notification) without mutating shared state.

nodes.name: createScanState
nodes.summary: Creates the initial empty scan-state object used by the event reducer.

nodes.name: applyEvent
nodes.summary: Reducer that folds one parsed event into the running scan state, tracking active launches by tool-use id and agent id.

nodes.name: toAgentJobs
nodes.summary: Converts the accumulated scan state's launch records into the final AgentJob[] list, computing duration from parsed timestamps.

nodes.name: readAgents
nodes.summary: Public entry point: reads an entire transcript file, runs the line-by-line parse/reduce pipeline, and returns the final AgentJob[] list.

nodes.name: analyze.ts
nodes.summary: Whole-file session post-mortem analyzer powering the /kaizen skill and the Analytics tab: computes billable/context token totals, per-tool cost, subagent cost breakdown, and error signals from a transcript.
nodes.languageNotes: Pure function design (analyzeSession takes a file path and returns a SessionAnalysis) lets the Analytics tab re-run it live on each request rather than caching a stale report.

nodes.name: num
nodes.summary: Coerces a value to a finite number, defaulting to 0.

nodes.name: toolResultText
nodes.summary: Extracts concatenated text from a tool_result content block (local duplicate of the same helper in agents.ts).

nodes.name: isErrorResult
nodes.summary: Detects whether a tool_result block represents an error via the <tool_use_error> marker.

nodes.name: userText
nodes.summary: Extracts plain text from a user message's content blocks.

nodes.name: analyzeSession
nodes.summary: Main exported analyzer: reads the whole transcript, tallies token totals per turn/tool, matches tool-use/tool-result pairs to compute per-tool cost and error signals, pulls in subagent data via readAgents, and detects user correction phrases as an accuracy signal.

nodes.name: usage.ts
nodes.summary: Fetches account-level 5h/weekly rate-limit utilization from Anthropic's private OAuth usage endpoint for the header progress bars; reads the OAuth token from the macOS keychain or credentials file and caches results with a non-blocking background refresh.
nodes.languageNotes: Synchronous getCachedUsageState() always returns instantly and fires an async background refresh when the cache is stale, so the 3s sessions poll never blocks on a network call to api.anthropic.com.

nodes.name: baseUrl
nodes.summary: Resolves the base URL for the usage API request, defaulting to api.anthropic.com but overridable via CLAUDE_USAGE_BASE_URL for tests.

nodes.name: readToken
nodes.summary: Reads the Claude Code OAuth access token from the macOS keychain via `security find-generic-password`, falling back to ~/.claude/.credentials.json; returns ok/expired/missing state.

nodes.name: tokenFromCredsBlob
nodes.summary: Parses a raw credentials JSON blob into a token state, checking claudeAiOauth.accessToken and its expiry.

nodes.name: toRateLimit
nodes.summary: Maps a raw rate-limit-window payload from the Anthropic API into the app's RateLimit shape.

nodes.name: mapUsage
nodes.summary: Pure mapper converting the OAuth usage endpoint's response (defensively handling both top-level and rate_limits-wrapped shapes) into UsageLimits.

nodes.name: fetchUsage
nodes.summary: Performs the raw HTTPS/HTTP request to the Anthropic OAuth usage endpoint with bearer auth and beta headers, handling timeouts and non-2xx responses fail-open.

nodes.name: refreshNow
nodes.summary: Refreshes the module-level usage cache by reading the token and calling fetchUsage, fail-open on any error so a rejected fire-and-forget call never crashes the process.

nodes.name: getCachedUsageState
nodes.summary: Synchronous accessor returning the last cached usage limits/status immediately, triggering a non-blocking refreshNow() when the cache is older than 60s.

nodes.name: types.ts
nodes.summary: The API contract shared by frontend and backend: defines Session/SessionDetail/AgentJob/SessionAnalysis/AnalyticsReport/ManagementIndex/ScopeConfig and other payload shapes exchanged over GET /api/sessions, /api/management, and /api/analytics.
nodes.languageNotes: Pure type-only module (no runtime code); cross-boundary imports elsewhere use `import type` to keep this a compile-time-only dependency with zero runtime coupling.

nodes.name: App.tsx
nodes.summary: Root React component that renders the top-level section tabs and switches between the Sessions, Management, and Analytics views, lazy-loading the latter two so their bundles don't affect the always-loaded sessions monitor.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses React.lazy + Suspense to code-split Management and Analytics into separate chunks, loaded only when the user opens that section.

nodes.name: App
nodes.summary: Persists the active section (sessions/management/analytics) via usePersistedState and conditionally renders SessionsView, or a Suspense-wrapped ManagementView/AnalyticsView.

nodes.name: SectionTabs.tsx
nodes.summary: Renders the top-level tab bar (Sessions / Management / Analytics) that lets the user switch the dashboard's active section.

nodes.name: SectionTabs
nodes.summary: Stateless tab-bar component that highlights the active section and calls the onChange callback with the clicked section name.

nodes.name: main.tsx
nodes.summary: Vite/React entry point that mounts the App component into the DOM root inside StrictMode and imports the global stylesheet.

nodes.name: .dockerignore
nodes.summary: Excludes node_modules, build output, VCS/IDE metadata, .env, logs, and OS cruft from the Docker build context so image builds stay fast and secrets never get baked in.

nodes.name: docker-compose.yml
nodes.summary: Production compose file: builds the default Dockerfile target, exposes port 4173, mounts the host's read-only ~/.claude directory for the dashboard to scan, and forwards runtime env vars (PORT, MAX_SESSIONS, CLAUDE_CREDENTIALS_JSON) for the packaged server.
nodes.languageNotes: Mounts the host's ~/.claude read-only since the container can't reach the host Keychain directly; CLAUDE_CREDENTIALS_JSON is expected to be exported via scripts/host-credentials.sh before `docker compose up`.

nodes.name: docker-compose.dev.yml
nodes.summary: Development compose file: builds the Dockerfile's `dev` stage, bind-mounts the repo for live editing while preserving the container's own linux node_modules, exposes both the Vite dev server (5173) and the API (4173), and mounts the host's read-only ~/.claude.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses an anonymous volume (`/app/node_modules`) to prevent the bind mount of the host repo from shadowing the container's installed dependencies — a common Docker Compose dev pattern.

nodes.name: Dockerfile
nodes.summary: Multi-stage Docker build with four stages (deps, dev, build, runtime) producing either a live-reload dev container or a minimal production image that runs the TypeScript server directly via tsx (no compile step) and serves the pre-built client bundle.
nodes.languageNotes: The `deps` stage installs dependencies once and is reused by both `build` and `runtime` via `COPY --from=deps`, avoiding redundant pnpm installs across stages; the runtime image never contains devDependencies-only build tooling since only `client/dist`, `server`, and `shared` are copied in.

nodes.name: deps
nodes.summary: Base stage that installs pnpm dependencies once with a frozen lockfile, reused by both the build and runtime stages to avoid duplicate installs.

nodes.name: dev
nodes.summary: Development stage extending `deps`; copies the full source tree and runs `pnpm dev` with the Vite and API ports exposed, intended to be paired with a bind mount from docker-compose.dev.yml.

nodes.name: build
nodes.summary: Build stage that reuses the installed node_modules from `deps` and runs `pnpm build` to bundle the Vite client into client/dist.

nodes.name: runtime
nodes.summary: Minimal production stage that copies only node_modules, package.json, server, shared, and the built client/dist, then runs the server directly via tsx on port 4173.

nodes.name: analytics.md
nodes.summary: Deep-dive rule doc for the Analytics tab: explains that `/kaizen` is the sole trigger for session post-mortems, how the server pairs the logged lesson with a live re-run of the deterministic analyzer, and the read-only invariant (no LLM calls, no write path).

nodes.name: management.md
nodes.summary: Deep-dive rule doc for the Management tab's three-pane config browser: documents the scope/endpoint model and the critical file-endpoint security invariant restricting served paths to the scanner's own enumerated set.

nodes.name: session-status.md
nodes.summary: Deep-dive rule doc for the session status state machine (the left status dot): the recent x turnComplete 2x2, the question override, the lsof process-liveness gate, Docker's skipProcScan default, and the empty-session filter.

nodes.name: usage-limits.md
nodes.summary: Deep-dive rule doc for the header's 5h/Week usage bars: documents the private Anthropic OAuth usage endpoint, keychain token lookup, fail-open caching strategy, and the removed token-refresh design.

nodes.name: view-persistence.md
nodes.summary: Deep-dive rule doc for Toolbar filter/sort persistence: documents the localStorage-backed usePersistedState hook, its fail-open shallow-merge behavior, and what is intentionally left ephemeral.

nodes.name: .env.example
nodes.summary: Template listing the dashboard's optional runtime environment variables (PORT, MAX_SESSIONS, ACTIVE_WINDOW_MIN, LOOKBACK_HOURS, SKIP_PROC_SCAN, context-window override, analytics toggles) with defaults documented inline for copying to a local .env.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Project overview and usage guide covering the live session monitor, Management and Analytics tabs, dev/prod/Docker run instructions, feature walkthrough (session rows, status dot, subagent detail, toolbar, usage bars), configuration options, and layout notes.

nodes.name: package.json
nodes.summary: Node package manifest for the dashboard monorepo: ESM module type, pnpm-managed scripts for dev (concurrent API+Vite), Docker dev, production start, tests, typecheck, and session-analytics, plus React runtime and TypeScript/Vite tooling dependencies.

nodes.name: skills-lock.json
nodes.summary: Lockfile pinning the two vendored Claude Code skills (frontend-design, vercel-react-best-practices) to their GitHub source paths and computed content hashes for reproducible skill installs.

nodes.name: tsconfig.json
nodes.summary: TypeScript compiler configuration (ES2022, Bundler resolution, strict mode, react-jsx, noEmit) scoping type-checking to shared/, server/, client/src, test/, and vite.config.ts across the monorepo.
nodes.languageNotes: noEmit + Bundler moduleResolution reflects the project's tsx-runtime-without-compile-step convention noted in the project docs.

nodes.name: config.json
nodes.summary: Tiny settings file for the Understand-Anything analysis tool, storing the single preference for output language ("en").

nodes.name: fingerprints.json
nodes.summary: Generated cache file from the Understand-Anything tool recording per-file content fingerprints (version, git commit hash, generation timestamp, and a files map) used to detect which source files changed since the last analysis run.
nodes.languageNotes: Auto-generated incremental-analysis cache; not hand-authored, so its size (2670 lines) reflects one entry per scanned project file rather than logical complexity.

nodes.name: knowledge-graph.json
nodes.summary: Generated knowledge-graph artifact produced by the Understand-Anything tool, containing the full set of nodes, edges, architectural layers, and a guided tour describing this project's codebase structure.
nodes.languageNotes: Machine-generated output (not source code) with distinct top-level sections for nodes, edges, layers, and tour, mirroring the GraphNode/GraphEdge schema this very analysis pipeline produces.

nodes.name: meta.json
nodes.summary: Small metadata file from the Understand-Anything tool recording when the project was last analyzed, the git commit hash at analysis time, the tool version, and the count of analyzed files.

nodes.name: CLAUDE.md
nodes.summary: Project-level instructions for Claude Code describing the dashboard's architecture (shared types, server domains, client components), dev/build/test commands, links to per-domain deep-dive rule docs, and coding conventions/gotchas.

nodes.name: .understandignore
nodes.summary: Gitignore-style ignore-pattern config for the Understand-Anything analysis tool, listing (mostly commented-out) suggestions for directories and test-file patterns to exclude from codebase scanning.

nodes.name: scan-result.json
nodes.summary: Intermediate machine-generated scan output from the Understand-Anything project scanner, capturing detected languages, frameworks, per-file metadata, import map, and estimated complexity for the whole repository.

nodes.name: index.html
nodes.summary: Vite HTML entry point for the React client; mounts the app into `#root` and loads `src/main.tsx` as an ES module.

nodes.name: styles.css
nodes.summary: Global stylesheet for the dashboard UI, defining the dark color palette as CSS custom properties and styling for tabs, toolbar, header, session list, and other layout primitives; kept verbatim from the original inline page to preserve class-name compatibility.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses CSS custom properties (`:root` variables) for a centralized dark theme palette consumed throughout component class rules.

nodes.name: agent-tracking-cache.md
nodes.summary: Design idea document proposing an incremental byte-offset cache for subagent tracking, covering the problem, a proposed offset-following design, caveats, and the core tradeoff to decide before building it.

nodes.name: session-and-agent-tracking.md
nodes.summary: Learning walkthrough explaining how the dashboard tracks sessions and subagents from on-disk transcripts: the two read paths (256KB tail vs. whole-file walk), session enumeration/ranking, the status dot state machine, and the lsof liveness gate.

nodes.name: 2026-07-06-usage-token-refresh-removal.md
nodes.summary: Post-mortem plan doc recording the removal of the in-app OAuth token refresh ('Sync') feature, explaining what was removed, why, current fail-open behavior, and guidance for a future Docker-compatible replacement.

nodes.name: 2026-07-01-usage-token-refresh.md
nodes.summary: Detailed multi-task implementation plan (later reverted/removed) for adding OAuth token refresh to the usage-bar feature, covering the API contract, server status plumbing, a spawn+single-flight refresh module, a refresh endpoint, a phantom-session filter, and client UI changes with end-to-end verification steps.

nodes.name: 2026-07-01-usage-token-refresh-design.md
nodes.summary: Design spec for the (later removed) usage-bar token refresh feature: problem statement, goal, decision, and design covering the API contract, server status plumbing, refresh endpoint, phantom-session mitigation, client changes, and accepted trade-offs/out-of-scope items.

layers.name: UI Layer
layers.description: React components and the app shell that render the Sessions, Management, and Analytics tabs.

layers.name: Client Data & State Layer
layers.description: React hooks and pure client-side lib logic that fetch/poll the API, persist view state, and provide filtering/formatting/entry-normalization utilities to the UI layer.

layers.name: Server Backend
layers.description: Node/tsx backend domain logic: HTTP entry point and API handlers plus the session-scanning, transcript-parsing, management-scanning, and analytics services they wire together.

layers.name: Shared API Contract
layers.description: The typed JSON contract and frontmatter parser shared verbatim across the client/server boundary, per the project's monolith-split architecture.

layers.name: Infrastructure & Build
layers.description: Container images, Compose orchestration, and the Vite dev-server proxy config used to build and deploy the dashboard.

layers.name: Configuration
layers.description: Project-level and tooling configuration files: package/TS manifests, Docker ignore/env templates, and the understand-anything analysis tool's own working state under .ua/.

layers.name: Documentation
layers.description: Project overview, per-domain deep-dive rules, and planning/idea docs that describe the dashboard's architecture and design decisions.

tour.title: Project Overview
tour.description: The README frames the whole project: a live, always-open monitor for parallel Claude Code sessions that reads ~/.claude/projects/*/*.jsonl transcripts off disk every 3 seconds — no daemon, no hooks. It also introduces the monolith-split architecture (server/, client/, shared/) and the two extra tabs, Management and Analytics, that this tour will explain in turn. Keep this mental model as the anchor for every step that follows.

tour.title: The HTTP Entry Point
tour.description: server/index.ts is where the backend actually starts: it wires up routing for /api/sessions, /api/management, and /api/analytics, and in production static-serves the built client. It has the lowest fan-in and highest entry-point score in the graph — nothing else calls into it, it's purely where execution begins. Everything the rest of the tour covers is reachable by following the imports that start here.

tour.title: The Shared API Contract
tour.description: shared/types.ts is the single most depended-upon file in the codebase (fan-in of 24) — it's the typed JSON contract that is the *only* thing allowed to cross the frontend/backend boundary, per the project's own architecture rule. Types like Session, SessionDetail, AgentJob, SessionAnalysis, and ManagementIndex/ScopeConfig defined here are what every server handler produces and every client hook consumes. Understanding this file before diving into either side of the split makes the rest of the tour click into place.
tour.languageLesson: Cross-boundary imports in this repo use `import type` so the shared contract has zero runtime coupling — the backend and frontend never actually execute each other's code, only agree on shapes.

tour.title: Request Handlers and Configuration
tour.description: server/api.ts implements the actual HTTP handlers that index.ts routes to, and has the highest fan-out in the graph — it wires together the scanner, management, and analytics modules into JSON responses. server/lib/config.ts sits right beside it, loading .env with process.env > .env > defaults precedence and detecting whether the app is running in Docker. Together these are the plumbing every feature in the next few steps flows through.

tour.title: Scanning Sessions and Reading Transcripts
tour.description: server/lib/scan.ts is the core of the Sessions tab: it enumerates every transcript under ~/.claude/projects, computes a live status for each, and ranks them for display. It leans on server/lib/transcript.ts, which tail-reads only the last 256KB of a .jsonl file to cheaply derive token usage, model, context window, and current tool activity — a deliberate performance tradeoff over parsing whole transcripts. The status-status.md rule doc spells out the exact state machine (the 2x2 of recent x turnComplete, the question override, and the lsof process-liveness gate) that scan.ts implements.

tour.title: Tracking Subagent Activity
tour.description: Where transcript.ts does a cheap tail-read, server/lib/agents.ts does the opposite: a whole-file parser and reducer that walks an entire transcript to extract Task-tool launches, tool uses, and usage blocks into AgentJob records — this is what powers the expanded per-session subagent view. server/lib/agents-cache.ts wraps that expensive whole-file walk with an incremental byte-offset cache, re-reading only newly appended bytes on repeat calls so the on-demand GET /api/sessions/:id endpoint stays cheap. The linked design doc explains the tradeoff that motivated building the cache in the first place.

tour.title: Account Usage Limits
tour.description: server/lib/usage.ts powers the header's 5h/Week progress bars by fetching account-level rate-limit utilization directly from Anthropic's private OAuth usage endpoint — reading the token from the macOS keychain or a credentials file, never the model-inference base URL. It's designed to fail open everywhere: no token, an expired token, or a network error simply hides the bars rather than breaking the dashboard. The linked rule doc documents this exact endpoint, the caching strategy, and a token-refresh feature that was deliberately removed as too much machinery for a cosmetic header.
tour.languageLesson: Fail-open design: every external dependency (keychain, network, private API) is wrapped so failure degrades a feature rather than crashing the request — a recurring pattern across this backend.

tour.title: The Management Config Scanner
tour.description: server/lib/management.ts powers the read-only Management tab by enumerating global, plugin, and project-level Claude config (skills, agents, commands, rules, hooks, settings) into a browsable index. Its most important job is security: it enforces a servable-path allowlist so the file-content endpoint can only ever return paths the scanner itself already enumerated — deliberately never a prefix/subtree check, since ~/.claude also holds credentials and history files that must never be served. This scanner has the second-highest fan-out in the backend, reflecting how many config sources it has to stitch together.

tour.title: The Analytics Post-Mortem Pipeline
tour.description: Three files form the read-only Analytics tab's pipeline: server/lib/sessionAnalyticsLog.ts parses ~/.claude/session-analytics-log.md (one line per /kaizen run) into structured lesson records, server/lib/analyze.ts is the deterministic post-mortem analyzer computing token totals and per-tool cost from a raw transcript, and server/lib/analytics.ts pairs the two together — re-running the analyzer live for each logged session rather than persisting reports. The /kaizen skill is the sole producer of entries in that log; the dashboard itself never writes anything, keeping the read-only invariant the linked rule doc insists on.

tour.title: The Frontend Entry Point
tour.description: Having covered the whole backend, we cross to the client side of the monolith split. client/src/main.tsx is the Vite/React entry point that mounts the App component into the DOM inside StrictMode, and client/src/App.tsx renders the top-level section tabs, switching between Sessions, Management, and Analytics. Management and Analytics are lazy-loaded here so their bundles never weigh down the always-on Sessions view, which is the default tab on load.
tour.languageLesson: React.lazy + code-splitting: wrapping a rarely-visited view in React.lazy defers its bundle to a separate chunk fetched only when the user actually switches to that tab.

tour.title: Live Polling and View State
tour.description: client/src/hooks/useSessions.ts polls GET /api/sessions every 3 seconds, the client-side twin of server/lib/scan.ts from Step 5. client/src/lib/filterSort.ts holds the pure filter/sort logic and default View shape the Toolbar and SessionsView share, while client/src/hooks/usePersistedState.ts is a generic useState replacement that transparently backs any state with localStorage, fail-open on bad JSON or a throwing storage API. Together these three files are why the dashboard's filters and sort order survive a page refresh without any backend involvement.

tour.title: The Session Row and Detail Drill-Down
tour.description: client/src/components/SessionRow.tsx renders a single collapsible row — status dot, project/branch/model, token usage — that expands into client/src/components/SessionDetail.tsx, which polls the on-demand per-session endpoint via useSessionDetail.ts to show the subagent timeline built back in Step 6. This trio is a tightly coupled cluster: filterSort.ts's shared constants and shared/types.ts's Session/SessionDetail shapes flow directly into how each row renders and expands.

tour.title: The Management Tab UI
tour.description: client/src/components/management/ManagementView.tsx is the three-pane view (scope menu, item list, detail pane) that wraps the server-side scanner from Step 8. client/src/hooks/useManagement.ts fetches the global index, a project's scope, and individual file contents with lazy per-item caching, while client/src/lib/managementEntries.ts is the pure logic that groups a ScopeConfig into a searchable, filterable entry list. No polling here — config changes over days, not seconds, so everything is fetched on mount or manual refresh only.

tour.title: Containerizing and Running the Dashboard
tour.description: The Dockerfile defines a four-stage multi-stage build (deps, dev, build, runtime) — the runtime stage runs the TypeScript server directly via tsx with no compile step, keeping the production image minimal. docker-compose.yml builds that default target for production, mounting the host's read-only ~/.claude directory so the containerized dashboard can still scan real session transcripts, while docker-compose.dev.yml builds the `dev` stage instead, bind-mounting the repo for live-reload while preserving the container's own linux node_modules. This is the capstone: everything from the server entry point in Step 2 through the frontend in Step 10 ultimately ships through these three files.
tour.languageLesson: Multi-stage Docker builds use multiple FROM statements so build-time dependencies (compilers, dev packages) never bloat the final runtime image — only the last stage's filesystem ships.
